channel.title: 🚀 Ultimate AI Feed - Authority First, Signal Over Noise
channel.description: Top 10 daily: semantic analysis, smart dedup, authority-first, quality over quantity.

item.title: [1] [大V/权威] How did the government decide OpenAI’s frontier model was safe to release?
item.description:
📊 Score: 192 | Category: 大V/权威

📡 Source: TechCrunch | Domain: techcrunch.com

  • Source:TechCrunch(+15)
  • ✓A-Domain:techcrunch.com(+30)

  • ⭐A-Authority:anthropic(+70)
  • ✓Industry-Gate:pass(+20)
  • real_release
  • TimeFactor:×0.8

📄 Summary: "Exactly what that dialog looked like between the government and Anthropic and OpenAI is unclear."...

item.title: [2] [模型算法] It Takes a MAESTRO To Prune Bad Experts
item.description:
📊 Score: 152 | Category: 模型算法

📡 Source: AlphaXiv | Domain: arxiv.org

✨ Why selected:

  • Source:AlphaXiv(+25)
  • 🔒S-Domain:arxiv.org(+50)
  • L2-Tech:pruning(+25)
  • L3-Tech:moe(+15)
  • L3-Tech:transformer(+15)
  • edge_optimization
  • TimeFactor:×0.8

📄 Summary: Sparsely-activated Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) language models achieve remarkable inference efficiency by activating only a small fraction of parameters per token, yet their full expert banks reside in memory at all times, creating a prohibitive deployment bottleneck. Existing structured pruning methods, largely designed for dense transformers, assess...

item.title: [6] [模型算法] BiSCo-LLM: Lookup-Free Binary Spherical Coding for Extreme Low-Bit Large Language Model Compression
item.description:
📊 Score: 124 | Category: 模型算法

📡 Source: AlphaXiv | Domain: arxiv.org

✨ Why selected:

  • Source:AlphaXiv(+25)
  • 🔒S-Domain:arxiv.org(+50)
  • L1-Tech:model compression(+40)
  • L2-Tech:quantization(+25)
  • L3-Tech:rag(+15)
  • TimeFactor:×0.8

📄 Summary: Large language models (LLMs) are increasingly constrained by memory capacity, weight bandwidth, and checkpoint storage during deployment. Existing low-bit compression methods mainly follow two directions. Scalar or group-wise quantization is simple and compatible with efficient low-precision kernels, but its representation capacity becomes limited ...
